target.title: feat: add scheduled cluster repair intake
target.body:
## Summary
- Migrate the Clownfish-style cluster repair lane into ClawSweeper using gitcrawl/gitcrawl-store cluster data.
- Add `repair-cluster-intake.yml`, a scheduled intake that runs every 30 minutes, reads `openclaw/gitcrawl-store`, imports at most one eligible cluster by default, publishes jobs plus `results/cluster-repair-intake/<repo>.json`, and dispatches through the one-worker `cluster_repair` lane.
- Gate only scheduled intake behind the repo variable `CLAWSWEEPER_FEATURE_CLUSTER_REPAIR_ENABLED=1`; direct/manual gitcrawl import and repair dispatch are not blocked by that variable, preserving compatibility for existing repair tooling.
- Record the processed gitcrawl-store portable DB SHA so repeated ClawSweeper ticks against the same store snapshot skip instead of dispatching duplicate cluster work. The upstream `openclaw/gitcrawl-store` refresh for `openclaw/openclaw` runs every 15 minutes.
- Keep cluster repair separate from the existing issue implementation solver: issue implementation handles individually triaged strict reproducible bugs, while cluster repair handles imported issue/PR clusters and low-signal PR cleanup candidates.
- Drip-feed imported gitcrawl cluster work by default: cluster import skips already-imported, security-sensitive, feature-request, and 75%+ closed clusters unless `--skip-closed-percent` is explicitly overridden.
- Preserve the existing repair/automerge/issue-implementation 40%-of-`workers.max` capacity derivation; only imported gitcrawl cluster and low-signal PR jobs use the new one-worker `cluster_repair` default lane.
- Reuse the existing repair worker/prompt/execute stack with `job_intent`-aware lane selection so migrated cluster jobs do not affect existing ClawSweeper repair behavior.
- Prefer portable `gitcrawl-store` SQLite state before the legacy local gitcrawl DB.
- Preserve explicit `CLAWSWEEPER_MAX_LIVE_WORKERS` / `--max-live-workers` override headroom up to the configured global worker cap.

## Enablement
To enable automatic cluster intake in a target repo, add the Actions repository variable `CLAWSWEEPER_FEATURE_CLUSTER_REPAIR_ENABLED=1`.

Live mutation still also requires the existing gates, mainly `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1` and `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_FIX_PR=1`. Merge behavior remains separately gated by `CLAWSWEEPER_ALLOW_MERGE=1` where applicable.

The optional `CLAWSWEEPER_CLUSTER_REPAIR_IMPORT_LIMIT` variable controls scheduled intake volume; default is `1` cluster per 30-minute tick. Manual dispatch remains available for debugging and can be disabled per run with `enabled=0`.

## Audit Notes
- Removed stale/self-contradicting docs that showed ordinary repair self-heal at the cluster one-worker limit; normal repair examples stay at the current 22-worker derived default and imported cluster examples use the 1-worker cluster default.
- Removed whitespace-only source churn from the PR diff. The remaining incidental source formatting in `execute-fix-artifact.ts` is required by `oxfmt`.
- `scripts/check-limits.ts` verifies both ordinary repair and imported-cluster examples, so the cluster docs check complements rather than replaces the normal repair docs check.
- `README.md` and `docs/limits.md` describe existing repair capacity as 40% of `workers.max`, not a fixed checked-in 22.
- `repair-cluster-intake.yml` includes the gitcrawl-store refresh-rate comment near the cron and skips repeated store snapshots by persisted SHA.

## Verification
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run check:limits`
target.author: romneyda
